task_completion.description: Navigate to the Appointment Types configuration page
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Oscar EMR interface with the 'Appointment Types' or schedule configuration administration page open.
Screenshots show 'Mental Health Intake' typed into the name or description field for a new appointment type.
Screenshots show '45' entered into the duration field for the new appointment type.
Screenshots show 'Red' selected from a dropdown or '#FF0000' entered into a color field for the new appointment type.

task_completion.description: Save and verify the new appointment type
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the newly created 'Mental Health Intake' appointment type in a list or table of saved appointment types, confirming it was successfully added to the system.

integrity.description: Agent used the GUI to create the appointment type
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacted with the Oscar EMR web interface using a browser, rather than using terminal commands, API calls, or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Oscar EMR application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the correct Oscar EMR URL (starting with http://localhost:8080/oscar/) and not a local file, text editor, or mock page.

integrity.description: Agent did not use developer tools to fake the result
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) being open or used to alter the page content to fake the success state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The appointment type must be named 'Mental Health Intake'. The duration must be exactly 45 minutes. The color must be Red, which corresponds to the hex code #FF0000. The Oscar EMR application is accessed via a web browser at http://localhost:8080/oscar/login.jsp.
